hero.title:
Vehicle Diagnostics System
& CAN bus Training
hero.desc:
An integrated platform with three components — embedded hardware, Android app, and web platform —
serving CAN bus protocol teaching and automotive diagnostics procedures for
Automotive Engineering students.
hero.imgAlt: CAN bus practice workbench at the Automotive Engineering lab

context.title: Why is this project needed?
context.p1:
Commercial vehicle diagnostic devices (Autel, Launch, Bosch ESI…) are expensive
and closed-source. Automotive Engineering students have limited access to the low-level
CAN protocol, unable to observe raw data frames or modify
decoding algorithms.
context.p2:
This project builds an open system for the laboratory: self-designed hardware using
readily available components, fully provided source code, accompanied by practice documentation
for 6 sessions from basic to advanced.
context.p3:
The implementation scope includes a Ford Ranger 2.0L Bi-Turbo (2018–2024) as the
test subject, supporting OBD-II standard and extending to UDS Mode 22 with Ford-specific
DIDs.

architecture.title: Three Integrated Pillars
architecture.sub: Hardware for measurement, mobile app for display, web platform for administration — connected via CAN, USB, and HTTPS.

hardware.title: HARDWARE
hardware.desc: Read & decode CAN frames from ECU

mobile.title: MOBILE APP
mobile.desc: Data logging, actuator activation, Lab mode

web.title: WEB PLATFORM
web.desc: User management and Lab system for instructors

hardware.title: Custom CAN bus Interface Board
hardware.sub: CAN ↔ USB converter with binary data frames, checksum validation, and auto-detect baudrate.
components: STM32F103C8T6 — Cortex-M3 MCU, 72 MHz, 64 KB Flash
MCP2515 — CAN 2.0B module with integrated transceiver, SPI interface
CP2102 — Silicon Labs USB-UART bridge, Android drivers included
Power supply — 12 V DC → 5 V (LM7805) → 3.3 V (AMS1117)
hardware.uartDesc:
XOR checksum across the PAYLOAD ensures transmission error detection. Fixed SOF/EOF
(0xAA / 0x55) allows quick re-synchronization on frame loss.
protocols: OBD-II Mode 03/04 — Read / clear DTC fault codes
Ford UDS Mode 22 — Read Data By Identifier (proprietary DIDs)
ISO-TP 15765-2 — Payload transmission >8 bytes over CAN

mobile.title: Android Diagnostic & Learning App
mobile.sub: Communicates with CAN board via USB-OTG, logs data, activates actuators, and supports Lab mode for classroom use.

features.title: Data Logger
features.desc: Log data to CSV file for offline analysis after test runs.

features.title: Actuator Test
features.desc: Activate actuators (EGR valve, fuel pump, electric fan…) via UDS Mode 31.

features.title: Raw CAN Monitor
features.desc: View raw CAN bytes alongside decoded results, a debugging tool for engineers.

mobile.labDesc:
When an instructor activates a session, the app automatically switches to Lab mode. Students
log in with their Student ID, take the pre-quiz, follow step-by-step instructions, then complete
the post-lab. All CAN operations are recorded as evidence and sent in real-time to the instructor
dashboard. PDF reports are auto-generated at the end of the session.

webPillar.title: Administration & Online Learning Portal
webPillar.sub: Web app for instructors to organize sessions, track progress, and export reports; for admins to manage users, activity logs, and CSV files exported from devices.
forTeacher: Create Labs & Sessions — Configure lessons, open sessions for classes
Manage student groups — Create groups, assign students
Real-time monitoring — View student evidence during lessons
Download PDF reports — Export end-of-session reports
forAdmin: User management — Create/lock accounts, assign roles
Activity Logs live — Stream login and CAN operation logs
CSV Exports archive — Download CAN log files uploaded by the app

useCase.title: How a Class Session Works
useCase.sub: A standardized 6-step process, from when the instructor opens a session to when the PDF report is saved in the system.

steps.title: INSTRUCTOR CREATES SESSION
steps.desc: Instructor selects a Lab, opens a new session, and the system generates a 6-character code.

steps.title: STUDENT JOINS
steps.desc: Student enters the session code on the app/web using their Student ID account.

steps.title: PRE-QUIZ
steps.desc: 10 multiple-choice questions testing prerequisite knowledge (5 minutes).

steps.title: PRACTICE
steps.desc: Connect USB cable → board → vehicle. Follow step-by-step instructions, evidence sent in real-time to dashboard.

steps.title: POST-LAB
steps.desc: 5 review questions after the session (3 minutes).

steps.title: PDF REPORT
steps.desc: System generates a report with pre/post scores, evidence, digital signature. Saved to "My Reports" and instructor's DB.

techStack.title: Technologies Used
techStack.sub: Technology choices prioritize open source, large community, and suitability for educational environments.

team.title: Project Team
team.sub: Graduation project academic year 2025–2026, Department of Automotive Engineering, Faculty of Transportation Engineering, Ho Chi Minh City University of Technology.
